title: STUDY TOPIC: - Nativity part 17
scripture_text: ‘’And Mary said: “My soul glorifies the Lord and my spirit rejoices in God my Saviour, for he has been mindful of the humble state of his servant.
devotional:
From now on all generations will call me blessed, for the Mighty One has done great things for me—holy is his name. His mercy extends to those who fear him,

from generation to generation. He has performed mighty deeds with his arm; he has scattered those who are proud in their inmost thoughts. He has brought down rulers from their thrones but has lifted up the humble. He has filled the hungry with good things but has sent the rich away empty. He has helped his servant Israel, remembering to be merciful to Abraham and his descendants forever, just as he promised our ancestors.”Mary stayed with Elizabeth for about three months and then returned home.’’

Mary was a worshipper.

Listen to what she says."Oh, how my soul praises the Lord.

This is amazing, Mary has just found out that her plans and reputation were going to be trashed. She been told that she was going to be responsible for mothering the Son of God. She did not know what Joseph her betrothed would do when he heard she was pregnant. Also, to claim that Yahweh, the God Israel caused her conception, would have been blasphemous as that is something the pagans believed their ‘god’s’ would have done. She knew that she was going to be misunderstood and judged, but instead of being overcome with worry and self-concern, she worshipped God.

Sometimes we sing ‘Come, now is the time to worship, Come, now is the time to give your heart, Come, just as you are to worship, Come, just as you are before your God.’ It is easy to sing this in church, but when you are facing the reality of an uncomfortable future, this becomes much more difficult, but probably more necessary.

Why?

Because when we worship God, we are reminding ourselves who He is, we are recognising his greatness and glory. It is in times of crisis, change and uncertainty that we need a more focused understanding of who God is. This fresh appreciation of God, is part of us preparing ourselves for the future.

"Mary's song’’ is called the Magnificat because the first word of it in Latin is Magnificat. Mary’s intense desire to magnify God became a determining influence that enabled her to accomplish God’s will for her life. The Greek word for praises is megaluno. It has two basic meanings. 1. To speak very highly of, 2. To show kindness. Mary had this deep hearts desire to speak highly of her God, and in doing so she was showing great kindness to God. For Mary, her recognition and praise of God in the present, gave her a confidence in God for the future. Here is a question we need to ask ourselves,

‘Is the exalting of God a natural part of our everyday conversation? Has it become our default language?
prayer: Father may my heart be always be filled with praise and gratitude for you,
html:
STUDY TOPIC: - Nativity part 17

Study Text: Mary’s Song Luke 1:46-56
‘’And Mary said: “My soul glorifies the Lord and my spirit rejoices in God my Saviour, for he has been mindful of the humble state of his servant.

From now on all generations will call me blessed, for the Mighty One has done great things for me—holy is his name. His mercy extends to those who fear him,

from generation to generation. He has performed mighty deeds with his arm; he has scattered those who are proud in their inmost thoughts. He has brought down rulers from their thrones but has lifted up the humble. He has filled the hungry with good things but has sent the rich away empty. He has helped his servant Israel, remembering to be merciful to Abraham and his descendants forever, just as he promised our ancestors.”Mary stayed with Elizabeth for about three months and then returned home.’’

Mary was a worshipper.

Listen to what she says."Oh, how my soul praises the Lord.

This is amazing, Mary has just found out that her plans and reputation were going to be trashed. She been told that she was going to be responsible for mothering the Son of God. She did not know what Joseph her betrothed would do when he heard she was pregnant. Also, to claim that Yahweh, the God Israel caused her conception, would have been blasphemous as that is something the pagans believed their ‘god’s’ would have done. She knew that she was going to be misunderstood and judged, but instead of being overcome with worry and self-concern, she worshipped God.

Sometimes we sing ‘Come, now is the time to worship, Come, now is the time to give your heart, Come, just as you are to worship, Come, just as you are before your God.’ It is easy to sing this in church, but when you are facing the reality of an uncomfortable future, this becomes much more difficult, but probably more necessary.

Why?

Because when we worship God, we are reminding ourselves who He is, we are recognising his greatness and glory. It is in times of crisis, change and uncertainty that we need a more focused understanding of who God is. This fresh appreciation of God, is part of us preparing ourselves for the future.

"Mary's song’’ is called the Magnificat because the first word of it in Latin is Magnificat. Mary’s intense desire to magnify God became a determining influence that enabled her to accomplish God’s will for her life. The Greek word for praises is megaluno. It has two basic meanings. 1. To speak very highly of, 2. To show kindness. Mary had this deep hearts desire to speak highly of her God, and in doing so she was showing great kindness to God. For Mary, her recognition and praise of God in the present, gave her a confidence in God for the future. Here is a question we need to ask ourselves,

‘Is the exalting of God a natural part of our everyday conversation? Has it become our default language?

Prayer: Father may my heart be always be filled with praise and gratitude for you,
Bible in a year reading plan: Job 1-3
